title: [security] missing X25519 clamping in key derivation
description: python/src/lichen/crypto/identity.py:99-100 uses sha512(self.seed).digest()[:32] for x25519_private without clamping (cf. _clamp in schnorr48.py:91-97 and libsodium crypto_scalarmult_curve25519). This is a key handling flaw relevant to EDHOC static DH and OSCORE key material. No constant-time guarantees from Python hashlib in hot paths. See RFC 7748 section 5 and draft-lichen-security. Commit 2984998b1 touched this code.
close_reason: Fixed X25519 clamping in identity.py, updated spec, tests, and exposed clamp function. All Python tests now pass. Commit a406e47.
